rationale: Reduce Windows CLI argument-length risk by centralizing limits and preferring file-based inputs for long payloads; this is a low-risk procedural config addition under allowlist.

edits.content:
{
  "schema": "brainflow.cli_arglength_policy.v1",
  "max_inline_chars": 2000,
  "max_arg_total_chars": 6000,
  "prefer_file_inputs": true,
  "file_input_fields": ["prompt", "context", "payload", "input_json", "instructions"],
  "notes": "When estimated CLI args exceed max_arg_total_chars, write payload to a temp file and pass a single file path argument instead of inline JSON."
}

risks: If consumers ignore this policy file, behavior will not change; minimal operational risk.
expected_benefit: Lower likelihood of Windows CLI failures due to argument length by encourself_evolution_stability_capability file-based inputs for large payloads.
